explanation: The natural language statement indicates that when the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot changes from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. This can be represented in LTL as: if state_is_TRANSITION is true, then when supported and good are true, eventually state_is_NOMINAL is true.

The natural language says that the autopilot state will change from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the system is supported and the sensor data is good. This can be captured by stating that globally, if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then in the next state, the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state.

The LTL formula 'G((state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good) -> X state_is_NOMINAL)' states that globally, if the system is in the TRANSITION state, is supported, and the sensor data is good, then in the next state, the system will be in the NOMINAL state.

The LTL formula 'G((state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good) -> X state_is_NOMINAL)' means that globally, if the autopilot is in TRANSITION and the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then in the next state, the autopilot must be in NOMINAL. This accurately captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the system is supported and sensor data is good.

The natural language describes a condition for changing states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL. Specifically, the state must change to NOMINAL in the next step (X) if the current state is TRANSITION, the system is supported, and the sensor data is good. This is captured by the LTL formula 'G(state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good -> X state_is_NOMINAL)'.

The natural language indicates that when the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, and both 'supported' and 'good' are true, then the autopilot will eventually be in the NOMINAL state. This is captured by the LTL formula G(state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good -> F state_is_NOMINAL).

The natural language indicates that when the system is supported (supported) and the sensor data is good (good), the autopilot state should change from TRANSITION (state_is_TRANSITION) to NOMINAL (state_is_NOMINAL). This can be formalized in LTL as: always, if current state is TRANSITION, then eventually the state will be NOMINAL, if the system is supported and the sensor data is good.

The natural language indicates that when the system is supported and sensor data is good while the autopilot is transitioning, then the autopilot will change to nominal. This can be captured using the implication operator. The atomic propositions 'supported', 'good', 'state_is_TRANSITION', and 'state_is_NOMINAL' are used to represent the conditions and state transitions.
